Define data preparation parameters: batch_size = 32, img_height = 224, img_width = 224, num_classes = 1000
Load a sample dataset using TensorFlow's datasets module for demonstration purposes (e.g., tf.keras.datasets.cifar10)
Preprocess the data by resizing and normalizing images to fit input requirements of ResNet50V2
Define the inputs to the Siamese model: anchor_input, positive_input, negative_input, each with shape (batch_size, img_height, img_width, 3)
Create sample triplets of embeddings using random data for demonstration purposes
Simulate the DistanceLayer by computing distances between the triplets and generating outputs in the form of ap_distance and an_distance
Instantiate the SiameseModel with a custom training loop
Define the triplet loss function within the train_step method to compute the difference between distances + margin
Compile the SiameseModel with the Adam optimizer and call fit() with the prepared dataset for a single epoch
Monitor GPU memory usage during training using TensorFlow's tf.config.experimental.list_physical_devices('GPU')
Assert that the GPU memory used exceeds an expected threshold to validate the bug (e.g., assert gpu_memory_usage > expected_threshold)
